title: 2015-2021 Lexus NX200t NX300h EPB Release - Rear Brake Service Mode, No Scan Tool Or Battery Needed
description: Activate electric parking brake service mode for rear brake pad replacement on 2015–2021 Lexus NX models using the DLC3 connector jumper wire method.
tips: Use a wire with insulation stripped on both ends or a metal paper clip to connect terminals 4 and 12 on the DLC3 connector.
Connect the jumper wire with the ignition off before starting the procedure.
On some models, the DLC3 connector may be installed upside down — on this model, pins 4 and 12 are counted from the left side, not the right.
This procedure applies to North American models only; other markets such as Europe and Japan may differ.
Do not touch the brake pedal when pressing the start button twice to turn the ignition on (except on newer models).
The pull/push sequence must be completed within 8 seconds of turning the ignition on.
On 2020 and newer NX models, if you get a 'release parking brake while depressing brake' message, perform the pull/push sequence with your foot on the brake pedal.
The parking brake warning light flashing rapidly confirms the sequence was accepted; flashing slowly confirms service mode is active.
Turn the ignition off before compressing the caliper and replacing the brake pads.
To exit service mode, turn the ignition on and pull the parking brake switch — wait for the warning light to stop flashing.
Remove the jumper wire from the DLC3 connector with the ignition off after the procedure is complete.
There is a small chance of setting a brake system fault code regardless of the method used — if set, it must be cleared with a good quality scan tool.
